title: lappula redowskii f. brachystyla
intro: Lappula redowskii f. brachystyla, also known as the dwarf button gromwell, is a flowering plant species within the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is primarily found in mountainous regions of the western part of North America. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, bronchitis, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by drying and processing the flowers and leaves to extract its medicinal properties.
